description_long: This object is a traditional Russian-style tea glass holder, known as a podstakannik, featuring a cylindrical black glass cup encased in an ornate, dark metallic holder. The metal holder is decorated with intricate floral and geometric patterns, and it has a slightly flared base for stability. A single, elegantly curved metal handle is attached to one side, allowing for easy gripping. The glass itself is smooth and glossy, contrasting with the matte, textured metalwork. Small perforations and decorative cutouts are present on the metal, adding to its visual complexity. The overall appearance is both functional and decorative, evoking a sense of vintage urban charm.
description: A black glass cup set in an ornate, dark metal holder with a curved handle and decorative cutouts. The design is both functional and visually intricate.
description_view_0: Front view shows the detailed floral pattern on the metal holder and the smooth, glossy black glass cup.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the curved metal handle and more of the intricate cutout designs on the holder.
description_view_2: Rear view highlights the symmetrical pattern on the back of the metal holder and the continuous cylindrical shape of the glass.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displays the handle from a different angle and additional decorative elements on the metal.
attribute_bowl_curvature: The bowl is straight-sided with a very subtle outward curve near the top, giving it a cylindrical appearance with a gentle transition at the rim.
